vitals.date_specific: One edition ever held: June 29-30, 2024. No 2025 or 2026 edition on the official UCI Gran Fondo World Series calendar.
vitals.field_size: ~1,800 total event participants (UCI-reported, across all formats, not necessarily road-race starters)
vitals.registration: Registration closed — event defunct after its single 2024 edition

climate.description: Late June on Ireland's northwest Atlantic coast: cool, changeable, wind- and rain-prone even in summer.
challenges: Precipitation and wind off the Atlantic are common even in late June

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 60. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 57. SOL REVIEW FLAG (2026-07-24, not applied — numeric dimensions locked per wave-10 rule): reviewer notes organization=4 is hard to reconcile with the documented council criticism (short-notice changes, poor communication, unfulfilled commitments), and scenic_experience=4 is weakly supported since Benbulben was tourism copy, not an actual course feature. Flagging for a human rescoring pass, not auto-applying.

course_description.character: The only edition of UCI Gran Fondo Ireland ran a closed-road, two-day format out of Sligo on the Wild Atlantic Way: a time trial plus a Gran Fondo/Medio Fondo built around a repeated ~54km inland loop through Strandhill, Ransboro, Ballysadare, Collooney, Coolaney, Ballymote, Drumfin, Riverstown and Ballygawley before returning via Carraroe to Doorly Park. It was rolling, fast bunch-racing terrain — 1,050m over 135km via repeated undulations and loop attrition, not a mountain gran fondo built around one big pass. Marketing leaned on Sligo's 'Yeats Country' scenery (Benbulben included), but Benbulben was never actually on the closed-road route.
course_description.signature_challenge: No officially identified signature climb — the challenge was the repeated 54km inland loop and its accumulated rolling terrain, not a single categorized ascent.
suffering_zones: The repeated inland loop through Ballysadare, Collooney and Ballymote — undulating enough to add up over two laps, without ever offering one defining summit
A pre-race route cut from an advertised 159km/1,176m down to the actual 135km/1,050m for unspecified 'unforeseen circumstances' — a late change riders had to absorb close to race day

logistics.lodging_strategy: Not specified in available sources. Sligo town will have standard accommodation options as a regional hub.

history.origin_story: Announced February 6, 2024 and inaugurated that June as the first Irish round of the UCI Gran Fondo World Series, organized by Titan Experience with support from Failte Ireland and Sligo County Council.
notable_moments: First and only edition, June 29-30, 2024, drawing roughly 1,800 event participants from 20 countries
Sligo County Council minutes credit council intervention with preventing the event from being cancelled outright amid organizational problems
history.reputation: A one-and-done UCI qualifier whose own local council went on record criticizing the organizer for short-notice changes and unfulfilled promises.

biased_opinion.verdict: One-Shot UCI Qualifier That Didn't Come Back
biased_opinion.summary: UCI Gran Fondo Ireland ran exactly once, in June 2024, as the series' first Irish round out of Sligo. It's gone from both the 2025 and 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Series calendars with no announced return and no cancellation notice either — it just quietly disappeared. What's on record about that single edition isn't flattering: Sligo County Council's own meeting minutes describe short-notice route and road-closure changes, poor communication with residents, Gardai and local businesses, a Tubbercurry volunteer contingent left stranded after their community was cut from the route, and the council's chief executive crediting council intervention with keeping the event from being cancelled outright. The UCI's own race-week course change — advertised 159km/1,176m courses cut to 135km/1,050m for unspecified 'unforeseen circumstances' — corroborates that the planning wasn't smooth.
strengths: Genuine UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier status for its one edition — real age-category racing, not a marketing label
Closed-road format on Ireland's Wild Atlantic Way coast, a genuinely scenic corner of the country
Drew a credible field for a debut event — roughly 1,800 participants from 20 countries in year one
weaknesses: Only ever held once (June 2024) — absent from both the 2025 and 2026 UCI calendars with no announced future edition
Sligo County Council's own minutes document short-notice changes, poor resident/business communication, and unfulfilled organizer commitments
The advertised course was cut significantly (159km/1,176m to 135km/1,050m) shortly before race day
No signature climb or defining terrain feature — a rolling, repeated-loop course rather than a marquee mountain gran fondo
biased_opinion.bottom_line: This profile documents a defunct one-off. If you're weighing whether to travel for it: don't — there's no evidence a second edition is coming, and the one edition that happened drew real organizational criticism from its own host council.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ireland's one and only UCI Gran Fondo — held once in 2024, then gone.
final_verdict.should_you_race: You can't — no edition has been scheduled since June 2024 and it's off the current UCI calendar entirely. Treat this as a historical record, not a race to plan around.
final_verdict.alternatives: For an active, currently-scheduled Irish road event: L'Etape Ireland (Carrick-on-Suir, ASO-organized, typically a September date — verify the current year's date directly). For closed-road UCI-series racing elsewhere, look to other active UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifiers on the current calendar.

youtube_data.search_query: UCI Gran Fondo Ireland gran fondo cycling Sligo

videos.title: The UCI Gran Fondo is coming to Ireland!
videos.description:
Calling all cyclists; the time has finally come...

The UCI Gran Fondo World Series is making its way to Ireland for the first time ever. Let the preparations begin, the countdown is on! 🚴🇮🇪

We are proud to announce that Sligo will host Ireland’s First Ever UCI Gran Fondo. We can’t wait to cycle the Wild Atlantic Way with you all on June 29th & 30th this year. 👏🏻

The global event that is for cyclists of all abilities will be coming to Sligo on the beautiful west coast of the country. A true Gran Fondo is meant to make an everyday rider feel like a fully supported professional rider, and Gran Fondo Ireland will do so with a real Irish flare.

Learn more and secure your place now at www.granfondoireland.ie

rider_intel.search_text: No usable rider-experience transcript content remains after purging two unrelated videos (2015 National Championships M40/M50 race, 2016 An Post Ras launch) that shared no connection to this event beyond generic Irish-cycling search terms.

eligibility.notes: UCI's own records show only the June 29-30, 2024 Sligo edition. Ireland is absent from the complete official 2025 and 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Series calendars, with no later registration or results found and no cancellation notice — the event simply did not recur. The database's inherited '2026: June 27' date, 152.9km distance, and '3000+ expected' field size are all unsupported third-party-listing figures and have been corrected to the verified 2024 race-week figures (135km/1,050m).
